title: Bottom tab bar: fix small tap targets + lean into Liquid Glass
description:
User report: the bottom navigation bar is not very responsive — you seemingly have to tap almost exactly on the glyph for the tab to switch, and roughly half of taps feel like they do nothing. Frustrating. User also wants to "lean into" Liquid Glass for the tab bar to make it look nicer, and pointed at the Axiom skill set for guidance.

Where: Sources/DesignSystem/LiquidGlassTabBar.swift (the custom bottom tab bar; EnoughTab cases Learn/Reviews/Progress/Browse). Rendered in Sources/App/MainShellView.swift. Related design system: Sources/DesignSystem/EnoughTab.swift (tab symbols/titles/axIDs).

Likely root cause of the dead taps (for conversion to confirm, not resolve here): in LiquidGlassTabBar.swift each tab is a Button (lines ~10-27) whose label is a VStack(image + text) with `.frame(maxWidth: .infinity)` and `.frame(minHeight: 58)`, styled `.buttonStyle(PlainButtonStyle())`. There is NO `.contentShape(Rectangle())` on the label. With a plain button and no explicit content shape, SwiftUI hit-tests only the non-transparent content (the ~18pt glyph + 10pt text), NOT the empty expanded frame around it — so the large-looking tab cell is mostly non-tappable and only a direct hit on the glyph/label registers. Adding `.contentShape(Rectangle())` to the label (so the whole maxWidth/minHeight frame is the hit area) is the classic fix; worth also sanity-checking the outer capsule padding and any overlay that could be intercepting touches. This is a hit-testing bug, not a gesture-conflict, most likely.

Liquid Glass state today: the bar ALREADY applies `.glassEffect(.regular, in: Capsule())` (line 37) plus a soft shadow — so it is partially on Liquid Glass already. "Lean into it" is an enhancement, not a from-scratch adoption: e.g. interactive glass (`.glassEffect(.regular.interactive())`), wrapping the tab items in a GlassEffectContainer, tinting the glass on the selected tab with the accentTheme, and/or a glass morph/selection animation. This is iOS 26 Liquid Glass (SwiftUI `glassEffect`), current deployment target of the app.

Axiom guidance the user flagged: relevant Axiom skills are axiom:axiom-swiftui (iOS 26 SwiftUI incl. Liquid Glass), axiom:axiom-design (HIG + Liquid Glass patterns), and the axiom:liquid-glass-auditor agent (scans for Liquid Glass adoption/migration opportunities) — conversion should consult these before shaping the visual-enhancement portion.

Scoping note for conversion (defer the decision): this is arguably TWO tasks — (1) a bugfix for the tap targets (small, high-value, unblocks usability — likely `contentShape` on the label), and (2) a visual polish/enhancement leaning further into Liquid Glass (subjective/taste — may warrant a needs-human review or a build-behind-a-toggle + pick-a-winner chain). Both touch the same file (LiquidGlassTabBar.swift), possibly EnoughTab.swift.
